rationales.achievement: He lets the postdoc present his framework as if it were the postdoc's own, failing to defend credit for work he developed.
rationales.power: He stays silent while someone else claims his seminar framework, giving up status and control over his own contribution in a public room.
He calls the coffee with Astrid the best part of the week because nobody is 'positioning themselves' or measuring publications and funding, valuing a break from status competition.

rationales.self_direction: He thinks his original framing is stronger but restructures chapter three around Bjorn's preferred lens because the target journal audience matters more than his own judgment.
rationales.hedonism: The chapter work leaves him with burning eyes, a messy flat, and meals reduced to bread and cheese, showing repeated sacrifice of comfort for the project.
rationales.achievement: He reorganizes the chapter for the journal they are targeting and keeps pushing through the evening, clearly prioritizing publication success.
He is willing to reshape citations and framing for better odds at the European Journal of Political Economy, explicitly optimizing for review outcomes.
rationales.power: He treats both his supervisor's name and selective committee information as leverage, using them strategically to protect his position against rivals.
He arrives with a prepared proposal and says he will keep pushing because he cannot accept just waiting for permission, showing a strong drive to influence how the department works.
rationales.universalism: His proposal is aimed at giving doctoral candidates more presenting time, advocating for broader opportunities beyond his own immediate advantage.

rationales.self_direction: When the committee delay blocks the Sciences Po application, he immediately thinks in terms of alternate routes instead of passively accepting the bottleneck.
rationales.achievement: He applies for the visiting researcher slot with polished materials and keeps looking for a way to preserve the opportunity.

rationales.power: He defends taking charge of the doctoral council, emphasizing that he reads everything, sets the agenda, and knows how to organize people.
rationales.conformity: He rejects the expectation that a doctoral student should stay smaller or less forceful, insisting he will keep taking up space even if others dislike it.

rationales.self_direction: He believes his case selection is stronger but goes along with Lena's preferred set after only limited pushback.
rationales.power: Lena's firm push and Bjorn's silence leave him without real control over the deliverables he will still have to answer for.
